derived.causal_summary: Added the two collectors packet 021 deferred to backfillRelationInference, both OPT-IN (default false) and inheriting the existing dryRun-default + bounded + created_by='auto' safety. Collector 1 (gated by options.similarity, default false) emits relation='supports' (strength ~0.35) by reading ONLY the pre-computed memory_index.related_memories column ([{ id, similarity }] on a 0-100 scale, written by link_related_on_save) — no live vector_search/sqlite-vec scan and no O(n^2) all-pairs work, so it is deterministic and unit-testable. It keeps neighbours with similarity >= threshold (default 75, configurable 1-100 via options.similarityThreshold), takes top K<=5, excludes self and any pair already produced by the spec-chain collector, and no-ops gracefully on absent/empty/unparseable column. Collector 2 (gated by options.contradicts, default false) emits relation='contradicts' (strength ~0.3) from structural memory_lineage.superseded_by_memory_id (predecessor contradicts successor) — NOT from embedding similarity, and it never calls detectContradictions (an insert-time guard) for candidates. Both collectors run inside the existing transaction and reuse the single post-commit invalidateEntityDensityCache(). Wired through schemas/tool-input-schemas.ts (similarity/contradicts/similarityThreshold), tools/types.ts CausalStatsArgs.backfill, and handlers/causal-graph.ts pass-through; the relation-coverage.ts honest hint now advertises the opt-in command. A new relation-backfill-similarity.vitest.ts proves opt-in-off-by-default, dryRun-zero-writes, bounded similarity (threshold/K/strength/auto), contradicts-from-supersession, idempotency, and graceful no-op. tsc clean; 166 tests green across 7 suites.
